text.title: docker-compose Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for docker-compose as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using `depends_on: [db]` without a condition or healthcheck.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This only controls start order, not readiness. The app starts the moment the db container process is launched, not when Postgres is accepting connections.

failure-mode.description: Using a bind mount for database data: `- ./pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data`
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bind mounts expose the host filesystem to the container, have permission issues (container UID vs host UID mismatch), and perform poorly on non-Linux hosts.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: `docker compose config` and `docker inspect` reveal these values to anyone with Docker socket access.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provides only startup ordering, not readiness. The dependent service starts before the database is accepting connections.

failure-mode.description: Building images in production compose files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Build context includes dev files (.git, test data). Use pre-built images from a registry in production.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Docker inserts an ACCEPT iptables rule before UFW rules, exposing the port to the internet regardless of firewall settings.
